title: Docker Buildx CI, Tests, Context, Cache, and Multi-Platform Output · Delivery-System Trace: Ci Docker Build Secret Cache Boundary
summary: A CI build sends the wrong Git context, ignores a generated file, leaks a credential through build args, trusts a warm cache, skips container behavior tests, and pushes before validation. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci docker build secret cache boundary increment in container build and test workflow. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use secret mounts, scoped cache backends, deterministic keys, trusted writers, and no credential-bearing ARG, layer, log, or attestation.
Configure official Docker setup and build actions with explicit Git or path context, Dockerfile, target, platforms, inputs, and output mode.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Buildx workflow leaks a secret through ARG, trusts a fork cache, builds only amd64, pushes before tests, and cannot tie output to source. Separate builder, context, platforms, cache, test, secret, and digest evidence. Competency probe: Use secret mounts, scoped cache backends, deterministic keys, trusted writers, and no credential-bearing ARG, layer, log, or attestation. A defensible solution applies Ci Docker Build Secret Cache Boundary, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to container build and test workflow.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-worked-ci-docker-build-test-cache-2
  - competency: ci-worked-ci-docker-build-test-cache-2
    artifact: container-build-workflow.yaml
    decision: worked ci docker build test cache 2
    environment: trusted release tag requiring an environment approval
    fault-injection: route five percent traffic to a revision with a data incompatibility
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked2
    change: |
      setup-buildx-action: 4
      build-push-action: 7
      context: explicit-path-after-generation
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
      platforms: [linux-amd64, linux-arm64]
      secret: buildkit-secret-mount
      cache-writer: trusted-main-only
      push: after-platform-tests
      build-record: retained
      output-digest: sha256-verified
    verify: render build inputs inspect build record test each platform changed config signals health and read-only behavior then verify index digest
    failure: Reject implicit Git context, ARG secrets, fork-writable cache, build-only proof, one-platform tests, or push-before-validation.
    repair: Select context deliberately, mount secrets, scope cache, test runtime behavior per platform, retain records, and push verified output.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Build arguments are an acceptable way to pass secrets because final ENV can be unset. This fails the Ci Docker Build Secret Cache Boundary contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
